content.full_text:
DRUG USE
IS ABUSE

MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL
KENNETH E FRIESS
JERRY HARRIS
GARY L HAUSDORFER
GIL JONES
JEFF VASQUEZ

CITY MANAGER
STEPHEN B JULIAN

July 10, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Formal Opinion - San Juan Capistrano

Gentlemen:

At the direction of the City Council of San Juan Capistrano, I am requesting a formal written opinion on the subject of a potential conflict of interest for two members of the City Council. At issue before the Council is a series of decisions involving the Solag Disposal Company and operations centered at the company's transfer station in San Juan Capistrano. The potential for a conflict of interest question arises because the transfer station is located within 300 feet of the real property of the Mayor (a Councilman) and within 2500 feet of the real property of another Councilman.

We enclose relevant materials, descriptions, documents, and a private legal opinion in order to obtain as conclusive an FPPC opinion as possible. Any other material you may require, including independent real estate appraisals of the properties, will be provided if requested. The issue has been tabled without a vote pending direction from the FPPC or its legal staff. This is a significant matter that may shape the future of the City of San Juan Capistrano for many years, and related issues are sure to appear on City Council agendas for several years to come. It is the desire of the Council to receive guidance that may be relied upon as conclusive on the issue whenever it arises.

NARRATIVE

On July 7, 1992, the City Council of San Juan Capistrano took up an item on its regular agenda to enter into an agreement to expand its exclusive solid waste franchise agreement with Solag Disposal Company to include residential curbside recycling for the next seven years, including automatic renewal thereafter.

32400 PASEO ADELANTO, SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO, CALIFORNIA 92675 • (714) 493-1171

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Two


The City Attorney advised the Council that two of its members should consider
abstaining on the item and withdraw from the discussion. This was done out of an
abundance of caution based upon the physical proximity of the Solag transfer station
in San Juan Capistrano to the business/residences of the two Council members.

One member of the Council, Mayor Jones, withdrew and left the meeting room. The
other Councilman, Jeff Vasquez, asked for a continuance of the item to allow for an
opinion from the FPPC. He expressed a strong desire to vote on the item and
questioned whether the Solag issue could result in an impact of $10,000 or more on
his business or property values. There was some discussion of the possibility of
retaining an appraiser to evaluate the potential value impact on the real estate, but
no decision was made.

The matter was postponed pending this request, and the City Attorney was directed
to seek a formal opinion from the FPPC.

ISSUES PRESENTED:

     A.   DOES MAYOR GIL JONES HAVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST THAT
          WOULD PRECLUDE HIM FROM PARTICIPATING IN DECISIONS
          INVOLVING CITY CONTRACTS WITH, OR THE RELOCATION OF,
          THE SOLAG DISPOSAL COMPANY TRANSFER SITE IN SAN JUAN
          CAPISTRANO?

     B.   DOES COUNCILMAN JEFF VASQUEZ HAVE A CONFLICT OF
          INTEREST THAT WOULD PRECLUDE HIM FROM PARTICIPATING
          IN DECISIONS INVOLVING CITY CONTRACTS WITH, OR THE
          RELOCATION OF, THE SOLAG DISPOSAL COMPANY TRANSFER
          SITE IN SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO?

FACTS PROVIDED:

1.   The Jones property at 31791 Los Rios Street, San Juan Capistrano, is both a
     residence and a business. The residence is an historical structure and miniature
     animal farm; the business consists of a petting zoo of farm animals and burro
     rides, with services that include on-site parties and events, and tourist
     services. The property is located approximately 250 feet from the nearest
     boundary of the Solag site.

2.   The Vasquez property at 31891 Los Rios Street, San Juan Capistrano, is both a
     business and a residence. The business is a photography studio. The property is
     located approximately 850 feet from the nearest boundary line of the Solag
     site.

3.   A diagram prepared to scale by City engineering personnel is provided to
     illustrate the relative locations of the properties. (EXHIBIT A.)

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Three


4.      The decision before the City Council on July 7, 1992, involved the approval of a
        lengthy contract extending solid waste and recycling services under exclusive
        franchise with Solag Disposal Company.    A copy of the Agreement is
        enclosed. (EXHIBIT B.)

5.      A matter for an agenda in the near future will involve the relocation of the
        Solag facility to another location in a different part of the City. Once again,
        the same properties will be involved, as well as the same City Council
        members. The issue will involve the removal of the Solag transfer station from
        the existing site, which will then be used for park purposes.

        The Solag transfer station site is described in an enclosure to this transmittal,
        which also includes an early appraisal of the Solag site for relocation
        purposes. (EXHIBIT C.)

6.      A copy of the memorandum from the City Attorney is enclosed. (EXHIBIT D.)
        Due to the frequency with which conflict of interest allegations have become
        the tool of community groups opposed to Council policies, the City Attorney
        has adopted the practice of scanning the agenda of City Council meetings for
        action items involving projects, contracts or properties within 2500 feet of real
        estate appearing on disclosure statements filed by City Council members. A
        memorandum is prepared for each meeting containing this information to alert
        Council members of possible conflict issues. No legal conclusions are drawn
        and participation is left to the discretion of each Council member.

7.      In January of 1992, a legal opinion on related issues was prepared by the law
        firm of Stradling, Yocca, Carlson & Rauth for the city and its Redevelopment
        Agency. This firm is contract counsel for the Redevelopment Agency. A copy
        of that opinion is provided with this transmittal. (The opinion also covers an
        unrelated issue affecting the same properties of the same Council members.)
        (EXHIBIT E.)

8.      A copy of the minutes of the July 7, 1992 meeting is also enclosed. (EXHIBIT
        F.)

9.      It should also be noted that on subsequent engineering review of property maps
        for the area, two additional property interests of two additional Councilmen
        were identified as lying within the 2500 foot distance from the Solag site.

        a)      Councilman Ken Friess disclosed "Condo Equity" in property at 31476
                Calle La Purísima, San Juan Capistrano, of 10% or greater, with rental
                income of "$1,001 - $10,000". This property is approximately 850 feet
                from the nearest boundary of the Solag site, however, it is separated
                geographically in a neighborhood containing no connecting roadways.

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Four


b) Councilman Jerry Harris disclosed a "Leasehold" interest in real property
located at 31441 Ave. de la Vista, San Juan Capistrano, of a value over
$100,000, providing income over $10,000 as a business of "Equestrian
Services". This property is approximately 1500 feet from the nearest
boundary of the Solag site, but again, is separated by geographical
boundaries in a neighborhood with no connecting roadways to the Solag
area.


DISCUSSION

Solag Disposal Company has an exclusive franchise with the City for all trash
collection services. The transfer site is the only location within the City limits
where Solag conducts its activities. The access road for hauling operations is Paseo
Adelanto. There is an acknowledged significant environmental impact on the
neighborhood from this activity.

Los Rios Street borders the downtown business district. It contains a mixture of
business, residences and combined business/residences. A plan has been under
consideration for many years to relocate Solag Disposal Company to another part of
the City. The plan would combine the present transfer station site with adjacent
land to form a City park.

The contract on the agenda would constitute a significant component of the Solag
operation. It is possible that the franchise could not continue without it. It is a long
term agreement with the City.

The properties in question are both combined business/residences. The Jones
residence is within 300 feet of the Solag property. (Regulation 18702.3(a)(1)) The
Jones business is tourist and activity oriented, and unrelated to solid waste hauling
except for the impacts on the property.

The Vasquez property is more than 300 feet and less than 2500 feet from the Solag
property. The residence is also a photography studio, a business unrelated to solid
waste hauling except for the impacts on the property.

No appraisal has been conducted of the impact of the Solag operation upon either
property. The City Council is prepared to do so if it will result in a conclusive
decision of the issue.

There has been a series of conflict allegations in recent years in this community and
a number of similar issues lie just ahead. San Juan Capistrano is geographically a
small community and it is difficult for anyone residing within the city limits to avoid
being within 2500 feet of any project of significant size. Naturally all elected
officials and all appointed commissioners must reside within the city limits.
Unfortunately, the population of 26,183 just exceeds the small jurisdiction threshold
of 25,000 in Regulation 18703.1. It is hoped that decisive formal direction from the
FPPC on early key issues such as this one will provide the necessary guidance to

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page Five


avoid repeated conflict situations and the inevitable stream of FPPC inquiries that
flow therefrom.

If we can provide any further information, please do not hesitate to call this office
immediately.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
RICHARD K. DENHALTER
City Attorney

RKD/ef

Enclosures
